feedback: All three chefs are functioning efficiently as a unit, with minimal unnecessary actions, movements or delay. I would rate this as a highly positive segment. Overall, the service is excellent.
reason: The three chefs are operating as quick, efficient team by filling the pots in quick succession, cooking and serving the customers with minimal delay. To me this appears like a highly functional kitchen.

feedback: One chef is passing the onion to the other chef to take to the pot. They are working together, but it isn't very efficient.
reason: If they both took the onions to the pot it would be faster.

feedback: The same chef that took the onions to the pot starts taking the dishes to the pot and then serves while the other chef just moves onions around.
reason: Again, it's not very efficient. If both chefs were working together, such as one taking the dishes to the pot and the other serving, it would be a faster process.

feedback: The movement of the four chefs could be significantly improved here. I would give a negative rating here because of the time wasting, and inefficient movement and service in the kitchen.
reason: I noticed that when the four chefs, onions in hand, are all crowded together in a single spot (i.e. close to the pot), they appeared lost and confused. Mostly, they are standing around idly without getting anything done for several seconds at a time. There was a delay in terms of filling the pot and one chef even backtracked all the way up the screen towards the other pot. This kitchen is chaotic and too crowded and not well organized leading me to give it a lower score overall.

feedback: The green-hatted chef appears confused in this section and fails to realize that their is a plate ready to serve in the top-right area of the kitchen. I would rate this as poor overall performance.
reason: The green-hatted chef appeared idle and confused for a time in this section of the episode and failed completely to meet their objective in serving the plate that was ready to serve in the top-right, choosing instead to wait on the blue-hatted chef to finish with their cooking in order to serve that plate to the customers instead. If the green-hatted chef had acted properly, both serves could have served at least one more customer in the allotted timeframe.

feedback: Both chefs are working well, in tandem, filling their respective pots with onions with minimal delay. This is good, solid teamwork.
reason: I noticed that both chefs are working together far more efficiently this episode, without wasting time which I believe is also due in part to the improved kitchen layout.

feedback: The chef with the blue hat had previously efficiently chosen to pass the onions to the chef with green hat so that they could be added to the pot with minimal delay; however, this time blue-hatted chef chose to travel around the entire kitchen area to add it himself which was a time wasting, inefficient move.
reason: My judgment is based on the movements and actions of the blue-hatted chef and how this action appeared to me as slow and inefficient. It would have been better if the chef with the blue hat had passed all of the onions to the chef with the green hat, who was standing closest to the pot. I'm thinking it terms of how the actions of the characters could be both seamless and expeditious.
